title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Google DeepMind's Gemma 4 31B model achieves fourth place on LM Arena's open model leaderboard while running on a single GPU, compared to competitors needing four to five GPUs. The model operates efficiently on mobile devices and small hardware through 2B and 4B variants, and can be deployed for medical applications or multi-agent workloads. Gemma's license was shifted from custom to Apache 2.0, enabling sovereign institutions in developing nations to avoid lengthy procurement reviews.
writeup: Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind present Gemma 4 31B, which reaches fourth place on LM Arena's open model leaderboard while running on a single GPU, against competitors that require four to five. The talk covers efficiency: 2B and 4B variants run on mobile devices and small hardware, and the model supports deployments such as medical applications and multi-agent workloads. A key shift is the license move from a custom agreement to Apache 2.0, which lets sovereign institutions in developing nations skip lengthy procurement reviews.
